ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "adminPrivilege" INTEGER /* Indicator of whether the employee has administrative privileges */,
  "username" TEXT /* Employee's username for system login */,
  "password" TEXT /* Encrypted password for system login */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of employee (e.g., doctor, technician, HR) */,
  "joiningDate" TEXT /* Date the employee joined the institution */,
  "education" TEXT /* Highest level of education achieved by the employee */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certifications held by the employee */,
  "languages" TEXT /* Languages spoken by the employee */,
  "id_superior" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's superior (if applicable) */,
  "id_specialty" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's specialty (if applicable) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Home address of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "hire_status" TEXT /* Current employment status (e.g., active, on leave, terminated) */,
  "contract_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the employment contract */,
  "contract_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the employment contract */,
  "work_location" TEXT /* Physical location where the employee works */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id_employee"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_certification FOREIGN KEY ("certification") REFERENCES certifications ("certification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_languages FOREIGN KEY ("languages") REFERENCES languages ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_adminPrivilege FOREIGN KEY ("adminPrivilege") REFERENCES administrative_privileges ("privilege_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_id_superior FOREIGN KEY ("id_superior") REFERENCES superiors ("superior_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_type FOREIGN KEY ("type") REFERENCES employee_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_education FOREIGN KEY ("education") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_id_specialty FOREIGN KEY ("id_specialty") REFERENCES specialties ("specialty_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "administrative_privileges" (
  "privilege_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each privilege level */,
  "privilege_level" INTEGER /* Level of administrative privilege */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the privilege */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("privilege_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the employee type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the employee type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "certifications" (
  "certification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each certification */,
  "certification_name" TEXT /* Name of the certification */,
  "issuing_body" TEXT /* Body that issued the certification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("certification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "superiors" (
  "superior_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each superior */,
  "superior_name" TEXT /* Name of the superior */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the superior */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the superior */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the superior belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("superior_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_superiors_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specialties" (
  "specialty_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specialty */,
  "specialty_name" TEXT /* Name of the specialty */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the specialty */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("specialty_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* ID of the employee accessing the module */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system module being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the module was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the module was accessed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_access_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES system_modules ("module_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_access_id_employee FOREIGN KEY ("id_employee") REFERENCES employees ("id_employee")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_modules" (
  "module_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system module */,
  "module_name" TEXT /* Name of the module */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the module */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being reviewed */,
  "performance_rating" REAL /* Performance rating on a scale from 1 to 5 */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance review */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who conducted the performance review */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments from the reviewer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_performance_id_employee FOREIGN KEY ("id_employee") REFERENCES employees ("id_employee"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_performance_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES employees ("id_employee")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_compliance" (
  "compliance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance record */,
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being reviewed */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy being reviewed */,
  "compliance_status" TEXT /* Status of compliance (e.g., compliant, non-compliant) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date of the compliance review */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who conducted the compliance review */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments from the reviewer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compliance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_compliance_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES institutional_policies ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_compliance_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES employees ("id_employee"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_compliance_id_employee FOREIGN KEY ("id_employee") REFERENCES employees ("id_employee")
)

CREATE TABLE "institutional_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_certifications" (
  "certification_id" INTEGER /* ID of the certification */,
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "issue_date" TEXT /* Date the certification was issued */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date the certification expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("certification_id", "id_employee")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the language */,
  "id_employee" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "proficiency_level" TEXT /* Proficiency level in the language (e.g., beginner, intermediate, advanced, fluent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id", "id_employee")
)
